# RS-MCA Proximity Prize Research

This is a dedicated Fable/Codex co-director workspace for RS-MCA proximity prize research.

Keep this lane isolated from Erdős 361, Erdős 346, and Random. Treat files in this folder as source/evidence for this workspace only.

3	Project: RS-MCA Proximity Prize Research
4	Status: Cycle 44 complete-audited; awaiting Danny/director decision on external role-split round vs local Cycle 45
5	Updated: 2026-06-19

35	The previous cosupport subset-product wall is now sharpened to symmetric-

58	
59	Working source snapshots:
60	
61	- `upstream_main_20260618/` is `origin/main` after Przemyslaw's manual integration commit `84b6dfa`.
62	- `current_repo_snapshot/` is the local pre-fetch snapshot containing the original PR #62 bundle.
63	- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/` contains the audited Cycle 1 Fable-loop material.
64	
65	Use `upstream_main_20260618/` first. Main papers remain unchanged; new material under `experimental/` is audit/experimental unless independently verified.
66	
67	Primary live route:
68	
69	1. F1 unrestricted same-numerator extension-line lift is COUNTERPACKET/ROUTE_CUT.
70	2. Fixed-rate sigma=1 extension counterexamples are banked as experimental/audit material.
71	3. Unbalanced extension denominators reduce to a residual list object.
72	4. Balanced monic-anchor denominator data reduce to a base-field `hat E = lcm(E,E^tau)` readout at effective prefix `<= 2 sigma`.
73	5. Cycle 1 banked a narrow quadratic reduction: arbitrary anchors factor through a paired base interpolation-residue readout modulo `Ehat=lcm(E,E^tau)`.
74	6. Cycle 5 sharpened the live gap to `W-F1-AA-RES`: prove or refute a reserve-indexed rigidity/value-count law for the paired readout on the bad line.
75	7. Cycle 6B recovered a clean structured Claude transcript after a malformed VS Code terminal artifact. Source audit shows the same-slope kernel `E*F_{<k}[X]` is only a tautological kernel/descent statement.
76	8. Cycle 7 recovered a clean structured Claude transcript after the terminal
77	   `response.md` lost spaces and duplicated repaint fragments. Source audit
78	   rejects the model's claimed `BANKABLE_LEMMA`: the nonconstant CRT multiplier
79	   `theta in B[X]/Ehat` cannot be absorbed into an ordinary pointwise word
80	   `w0+theta*w1`, so `thm:exactcount` does not transfer verbatim. The live
81	   target is now `W-F1-AA-RES-TWISTED-READOUT`.
82	9. Cycle 8 proves that the twist is isomorphic to the original extension
83	   residue count: `B[X]/Ehat ~= F[X]/E` and
84	   `[interp_S(w0)]_Ehat + theta [interp_S(w1)]_Ehat` maps to
85	   `[interp_S(w0)+alpha interp_S(w1)]_E`.
86	10. Cycle 9 proves the locator-quotient decomposition
87	   `W=L_S Q_S+interp_S(w)`, `deg Q_S<=n-a-1`, and cuts raw residue
88	   cardinality as the wrong MCA object. The live target is the bad-line
89	   incidence count
90	   `#{z in F : exists S, [interp_S(w)]_E=z[Bnum]_E}`.
91	11. Cycle 11 closes the first finite line-incidence regime
92	   `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=r-t=2`. The quotient has the closed form
93	   `Q_S=C(X-s_T)+C1`, bad-line landing is one conic `det(s_T,p_T)=0`, and
94	   the conditional slope count is `C2=O(n)` with generic `C2<=4`. This is a
95	   BANKABLE_LEMMA / AUDIT only for the low regime, not a proof of `conj:B`.
96	12. Cycle 12 gives the `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=r-t=3` quotient/quadric
97	    calculation. `Q_S` depends on `e1(T),e2(T)` and not `e3(T)`, while
98	    bad-line landing is a quadric in `(e1,e2,e3)` with leading coefficient
99	    `wedge([W]_E,[Bnum]_E)`. It does not prove a `C2` bound; the next wall is
100	    slope-fiber collapse on the split-cubic locus.
101	13. Cycle 13 proves the base-component complete-intersection route off
102	    resonance: `Delta=Res(L_T,E)*([I_S]_E wedge [Bnum]_E)`, the resultant is
103	    nonzero on valid co-supports, and after splitting
104	    `Delta=Delta_0+alpha Delta_1`, the two base quadrics are coprime off
105	    `R0 union Ra union Rb`. Therefore `C2=O(p)=O(n)` for `D=F_p`,
106	    `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, away from the classified resonance strata. The
107	    remaining target is the resonance locus where the base quadrics share a
108	    surface component.
109	14. Cycle 14 shows the resonance strata are not excluded by the currently
110	    banked source hypotheses and reduces the residual problem to the explicit
111	    slope equations
112	    `q1 z^2-(p1-q2)z-p2=0`, `tau_3=p1-zq1 in B` on a base surface
113	    `Sigma subset B^3`. The live wall is to prove `O(p)` slope image on
114	    every source-valid resonance surface or construct a source-valid
115	    `Theta(p^2)` slope counterpacket.
116	15. Cycle 15 sharpens that surface slope problem to the affine equation
117	    `L_z(tau)=iota-z mu=0` in `A=F[X]/E`. The `B`-linear columns
118	    `c1(z),c2(z),c3(z)` and determinant consistency polynomial
119	    `Q(z_0,z_1)=det[c1|c2|c3|c0]` are now the live invariant. Rank `3` alone
120	    is not enough: rank `3` with `Q!=0` gives a curve-sized slope set, while
121	    rank `3` with `Q==0` identically is the possible `Theta(q_line)` regime.
122	16. Cycle 16 banks the safe side: off `R0`, if `Q` is not identically zero,
123	    then `C2<=4p=O(p)=O(n)` in the `D=F_p`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3` test regime.
124	    It sharpens the residual wall to the `Q==0` branch restricted to distinct
125	    `D`-split cubics. The proposed trace/Gram criterion for `Q==0` is useful
126	    but remains AUDIT until checked by a local symbolic/finite verifier.
127	
128	17. Cycle 17 attempted a rank-det split scanner but produced no clean
129	    `response.md` and no `output_files/` deliverable. It remains AUDIT only.
130	18. Cycle 18 banks monicity of the `tau_3` determinant in the
131	    `{[W]_E,b}` basis and sharpens the residual branch to resonance
132	    slope-map collapse.
133	19. Cycle 19 proposes rank-one/gate formulas but is AUDIT only.
134	20. Cycle 20 banks the rank-one leading coefficient, quadric-branch normal
135	    form, and the `D` gate `det M=(c_b/kappa^2)D`.
136	21. Cycle 21 banks differential collapse gates: simultaneous branch collapse
137	    is governed by `J_A=J_Aprime=0`, and the resultant of those gates is the
138	    Cycle 20 gate `D`. It does not bank the stronger independence claim.
139	22. Cycle 22 banks the decoupling identity
140	    `Im_alpha(J_A)=2 Im_alpha(d)+Im_alpha(c) f_2` on `Delta1==0`. Therefore,
141	    on the explicit stratum `c in B`, `d notin B`, `Delta1==0`, `D=0`,
142	    alignment is impossible in odd characteristic if that stratum is
143	    source-valid and nonempty. It does not bank nonemptiness or a
144	    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
145	23. Cycle 23 proves the Cycle 22 `c in B`, `d notin B` nonemptiness wall is
146	    empty. In that branch `ell=[X^p-X]_E=mu*(xi+c/2)` and
147	    `D=-mu^2(c^2/4-d)kappa`, so `D!=0` off `R0`. The only surviving separated
148	    `D=0` lane is now `c notin B`.
149	24. Cycle 24 proves the norm factorization
150	    `D=N(ell)kappa`, with
151	    `ell=[X^p-X]_E=mu*(xi+c/2)+delta_c` and
152	    `N(ell)=prod_{a in F_p}E(a)`. Since source-valid denominators are nonzero
153	    on `D=F_p`, `N(ell)!=0`; off `R0`, `kappa!=0`; hence the entire
154	    source-valid `D=0`, off-`R0` branch is empty in this restricted
155	    `t=2,j=3` calculation. The live branch returns to the non-`D=0`
156	    determinant-split `Q==0` wall.
157	25. Cycle 25 banks the six-term Plucker/Laplace expansion of the
158	    slope-fiber determinant `Q(z)` and confirms that the `z`-free determinant
159	    gate `det M=(c_b/kappa^2)D` is separate from `Q(z)`. It also cuts the
160	    false route `Q(z)=0 iff realized slope`: `Q(z)=0` is sufficient only when
161	    the coefficient columns `C(z)=[c_1(z)|c_2(z)|c_3(z)]` have rank `3`; on
162	    rank-drop strata the augmented-rank minors of `[C(z)|c_0(z)]` are required.
163	26. Cycle 26 banks the DEP/NONDEP rank dichotomy. In NONDEP, rank-drop costs
164	    only `O(p)` slopes; away from rank-drop, `Q(z)=0` is necessary and
165	    sufficient for affine `tau in B^3` consistency. The `c notin B`,
166	    `c_b != 0` branch is NONDEP by leading-symbol independence. The proposed
167	    top-degree `Q_4` obstruction is a high-value next target but remains
168	    audit-only until independently verified, and the distinct split-cubic gate
169	    remains separate from affine consistency.
170	27. Cycle 27 identifies a candidate corrected top coefficient
171	    `Q_4=N(c_b)*(Im(d)^2-Im(c)*Im(conj(c)d))`. For `c notin B`, it factors
172	    as `N(c_b)*Im(c)^2*E(-Im(d)/Im(c))`, so `Q_4=0` iff `E` has an `F_p`
173	    root iff the Cycle 24 locator norm `prod_{a in F_p}E(a)` vanishes.
174	    For `c in B`, it becomes `N(c_b)*Im(d)^2`, nonzero under separatedness.
175	    This appears to cut the restricted source-valid `Q==0` branch, but it is
176	    banked only as `BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / AUDIT` until Cycle 28
177	    independently rederives the column algebra.
178	28. Cycle 28 independently confirms the `q1/q2` closed forms from the Cycle 20
179	    wedge identities and verifies the `Q_4` formula. The restricted
180	    `D=F_p`, `B=F_p`, `F=F_{p^2}`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, off-`R0`,
181	    `c_b!=0`, source-valid separated branch is now closed as a local
182	    `PROOF / ROUTE_CUT / AUDIT`: `Q` is never identically zero, so Cycle 16
183	    gives `C2<=4p=O(p)`. This is not a corrected-reserve, MCA, protocol, or
184	    prize theorem.
185	29. Cycle 29 shows that the Cycle 28 determinant mechanism changes meaning at
186	    `t=2,j=4`. The quotient depends on `tau_1,tau_2,tau_3`, while the
187	    locator residue introduces `tau_4`, giving a square affine system
188	    `M(z)tau=-C_0(z)` in the four-dimensional `B`-space `A=F[X]/E`. The top
189	    symbol persists as `TopSym(det_B M(z))=-N(kappa)N(z)^2Q_4`, with the
190	    same source-valid nonzero Cycle 28 locator quantity, but this only proves
191	    generic invertibility. It does not imply an `O(p)` slope bound for `j=4`.
192	    The live wall is the split-quartic gate for the rational preimage
193	    `tau(z)`.
194	30. Cycle 30 reduces the `t=2,j=4` split-quartic gate to the explicit
195	    one-`F`-quadric / two-`B`-quadric equation
196	    `Phi(tau)=iota wedge_F mu=0`, equivalently
197	    `kappa*N(lambda)-(ell[Q_S]_E) wedge_F (b lambda)=0`, with
198	    `lambda=[L_T]_E`. This banks the gate reduction only. Codex direct
199	    finite scans for random small source-valid instances lean toward
200	    `O(p)`-scale realized slope counts (`p=29` average about `1.08p`, not a
201	    positive-density fraction of `p^2`). The live wall is now to explain this
202	    split-locus collapse or construct a genuine source-valid `Theta(q_line)`
203	    growing-prime family.
204	31. Cycle 31 challenges the Cycle 30 scan interpretation and proposes the
205	    quartic monodromy invariant as the right object. Codex audit does not bank
206	    the claim that `O(p)` collapse is impossible and does not bank a
207	    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket. It does bank the next exact wall:
208	    compute/source-audit `disc_X L_{tau(z)}`, the resolvent cubic, the correct
209	    base-field model for `z in F_{p^2}` with splitting over `B=F_p`, and the
210	    geometric monodromy group.
211	32. Cycle 32 cuts the one-variable `B(z)` / `F(z)` framing. The correct model
212	    is a two-dimensional `B`-surface over `A^2_B`, with
213	    `z=z_0+alpha z_1`. Codex added a local Cramer-system factorization
214	    histogram checker. The checker matches direct support enumeration away
215	    from the determinant curve `Delta=0`; discrepancies are recorded as
216	    `singular_split_C2`. This is `ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL /
217	    AUDIT`, not proof of `S_4`, not proof of positive density, and not a
218	    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
219	33. Cycle 33 banks the singular determinant curve bound. Under the Cycle 29
220	    top-symbol nonzero hypotheses, `Delta(z_0,z_1)=det_B M(z)` has degree at
221	    most four and is not identically zero, so `Delta=0` contributes at most
222	    `4p=O(p)` split slopes. This is a restricted `BANKABLE_LEMMA /
223	    CONDITIONAL / AUDIT`, not a proof of off-curve positive density or a
224	    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
225	34. Cycle 34 banks the off-curve dominance lemma. On the source-valid dense
226	    open of the restricted `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` branch, the rational map
227	    `psi:z |-> tau(z)=M(z)^(-1)(-C_0(z))` has generic `B`-Jacobian rank two
228	    and is birational onto the Cycle 30 quadric image. This cuts the hidden
229	    rank-one / curve-collapse route for `O(p)` off-curve counts. It does not
230	    prove geometric `S_4`, positive split density, arithmetic/geometric
231	    monodromy equality, or a `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket. The live wall is
232	    the geometric monodromy and constant-field test.
233	35. Cycle 35 banks a finite-place S4 certificate. For any fixed source-valid
234	    restricted `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` instance, off-`Delta` squarefree
235	    factorization types `"4"` and `"13"` certify `G_arith=S_4`, and the even
236	    type `"13"` cuts the sign constant-field obstruction, giving
237	    `G_geom=G_arith=S_4` for that instance. This turns the Cycle 32 finite
238	    histogram into serious `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence, but it is not a
239	    uniform-in-`p` theorem and not a `COUNTERPACKET`. The live wall is now to
240	    construct an explicit source-valid growing-prime family with uniform S4,
241	    or identify the exact obstruction.
242	36. Cycle 36 banks an explicit source-valid growing-prime denominator family
243	    for primes `p = 3 mod 4`: `B=F_p`, `F=F_{p^2}=B(alpha)`,
244	    `alpha^2=-1`, `E=X^2+alpha X+1`, `b=X`. It reduces uniform geometric S4
245	    to a single-prime finite certificate after good-reduction monotonicity.
246	    Codex corrected the raw shortcut: quartic transitivity/geometric
247	    irreducibility must be certified in addition to resolvent irreducibility
248	    and discriminant nonsquareness. This is not a COUNTERPACKET and not an
249	    MCA/protocol/prize theorem.
250	37. Cycle 37 re-checks the source-valid hand gates for the Cycle 36 explicit
251	    family: `E(a)=(a^2+1)+alpha*a` has no `F_p` root, `E` and `E^tau` are
252	    separated on `D=F_p`, `c=alpha` gives the `c notin B`, `c_b!=0` branch,
253	    and choosing `u=1+X` gives `kappa=1`. With `c=alpha`, `d=1`, the top
254	    symbol quantity remains `Q4=N(c_b)!=0`, so `Delta=0` contributes at most
255	    `4p` slopes in this restricted branch. Cycle 37 did not produce a working
256	    single-prime S4 certificate: the supplied Python checker is type-malformed
257	    and fails by mixing `F`-elements with residue pairs. Status:
258	    `BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT`, not `PROOF`
259	    and not `COUNTERPACKET`.
260	38. Cycle 38 identifies and repairs the exact Cycle 37 checker type error.
261	    The repaired free data are actual `F`-elements
262	    `W_{n-1..n-4}=1,alpha,1+alpha,1`, not residue-pairs. Codex ran the
263	    patched checker locally at `p=31`; it returns
264	    `PASS_S4_finite_place=true` with factorization witnesses `"4"` and
265	    `"13"`, a nonsquare-discriminant witness, and no singular points on the
266	    tested line. This is finite-place `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence plus a
267	    bankable checker repair. It is not a uniform theorem, not geometric `S_4`
268	    over `B(z_0,z_1)`, not a `COUNTERPACKET`, and not an MCA/protocol/prize
269	    theorem.
270	39. Cycle 39 proves the locator-collapse lemma in the explicit `t=2,j=4`
271	    family: `[X^p-X]_E=alpha` when `(-5/p)=+1`, and
272	    `[X^p-X]_E=-2X` when `(-5/p)=-1`. Thus the family splits into two fixed
273	    congruence subcases instead of varying freely with `p`. The `p=31`
274	    certificate covers only Subcase B; Subcase A and good reduction remain
275	    open.
276	
277	Active target:
278	
279	- Cycle 41 homerun characteristic-zero Delta / good-reduction bridge:
280	  `W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-CHAR0DELTA`. The target is to bridge the
281	  finite-place geometric `S_4` certificates from Cycle 40 to characteristic
282	  zero and a growing-prime route, or identify the exact algebraic obstruction.
283	- Secondary target: `W-F1-AA-RES-T3J2`.
284

286

288	  `/Users/danielcabezas/packy-fable-ui/projects/rs-mca-proximity-prize-research/runs/2026-06-18T20-48-33-469Z-cycle41-homerun-char0delta-goodred-79f7afb3`
289	- Target: `W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-CHAR0DELTA`.

# Director State

Project: RS-MCA Proximity Prize Research
Status: Cycle 44 complete-audited; awaiting Danny/director decision on external role-split round vs local Cycle 45
Updated: 2026-06-19

Current target: `W-F1-AA-RES-SYMMETRIC-FUNCTION-CANCELLATION-L2-ANTICOLLISION`.

Cycle 42 external 5.5 Pro round conservatively banks the fixed restricted
`t=2,j=4` branch as a local closure:

```text
q_gen = p,
B = F_p,
F = F_{p^2},
q_line = p^2,
D = F_p,
t = sigma = 2,
j = 4,
N_split(p)=p^2/24+O(p^(3/2)).
```

This cuts the false A-side route `raw G2/G3 failure => Subcase A cannot
globalize`. It is not a corrected-reserve, fixed-rate, generated-field,
MCA/list/line-decoding/curve-MCA, protocol, SNARK, prize, or final
`COUNTERPACKET` theorem.

Cycle 43 completed and is audited as `BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT /
EXACT_NEW_WALL / AUDIT / EXPERIMENTAL`. It cuts the literal route of directly
amplifying the fixed `t=2,j=4` quartic/S4 mechanism to reserve scale.

Cycle 44 completed and is audited as `BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / AUDIT
/ EXPERIMENTAL`. It banks the exact cosupport moment identity
`rho(T)=-ell Lambda(T)^(-1)N(T)` and the exact landing orthogonality formula.
The previous cosupport subset-product wall is now sharpened to symmetric-
function cancellation, especially the L2 anticollision bound for `M_2`.

Active run:
None. Do not launch Cycle 45 until the Cycle 44 external packet / local prompt
choice is made.

Last completed run directory:

```text
/Users/danielcabezas/packy-fable-ui/projects/rs-mca-proximity-prize-research/runs/2026-06-19T00-05-26-501Z-cycle44-cosupport-subset-product-homerun-11471e45
```

```text
current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/prompts/20260619_cycle44_cosupport_subset_product_homerun.md
```

Cycle 44 response and run receipts were preserved under
`experimental/2026-06-18-fable-loop/raw/cycle44_cosupport_subset_product_homerun/`;
audit path:
`experimental/2026-06-18-fable-loop/audits/20260619_CYCLE44_COSUPPORT_MOMENT_IDENTITY_AUDIT.md`.

Working source snapshots:

- `upstream_main_20260618/` is `origin/main` after Przemyslaw's manual integration commit `84b6dfa`.
- `current_repo_snapshot/` is the local pre-fetch snapshot containing the original PR #62 bundle.
- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/` contains the audited Cycle 1 Fable-loop material.

Use `upstream_main_20260618/` first. Main papers remain unchanged; new material under `experimental/` is audit/experimental unless independently verified.

Primary live route:

1. F1 unrestricted same-numerator extension-line lift is COUNTERPACKET/ROUTE_CUT.
2. Fixed-rate sigma=1 extension counterexamples are banked as experimental/audit material.
3. Unbalanced extension denominators reduce to a residual list object.
4. Balanced monic-anchor denominator data reduce to a base-field `hat E = lcm(E,E^tau)` readout at effective prefix `<= 2 sigma`.
5. Cycle 1 banked a narrow quadratic reduction: arbitrary anchors factor through a paired base interpolation-residue readout modulo `Ehat=lcm(E,E^tau)`.
6. Cycle 5 sharpened the live gap to `W-F1-AA-RES`: prove or refute a reserve-indexed rigidity/value-count law for the paired readout on the bad line.
7. Cycle 6B recovered a clean structured Claude transcript after a malformed VS Code terminal artifact. Source audit shows the same-slope kernel `E*F_{<k}[X]` is only a tautological kernel/descent statement.
8. Cycle 7 recovered a clean structured Claude transcript after the terminal
   `response.md` lost spaces and duplicated repaint fragments. Source audit
   rejects the model's claimed `BANKABLE_LEMMA`: the nonconstant CRT multiplier
   `theta in B[X]/Ehat` cannot be absorbed into an ordinary pointwise word
   `w0+theta*w1`, so `thm:exactcount` does not transfer verbatim. The live
   target is now `W-F1-AA-RES-TWISTED-READOUT`.
9. Cycle 8 proves that the twist is isomorphic to the original extension
   residue count: `B[X]/Ehat ~= F[X]/E` and
   `[interp_S(w0)]_Ehat + theta [interp_S(w1)]_Ehat` maps to
   `[interp_S(w0)+alpha interp_S(w1)]_E`.
10. Cycle 9 proves the locator-quotient decomposition
   `W=L_S Q_S+interp_S(w)`, `deg Q_S<=n-a-1`, and cuts raw residue
   cardinality as the wrong MCA object. The live target is the bad-line
   incidence count
   `#{z in F : exists S, [interp_S(w)]_E=z[Bnum]_E}`.
11. Cycle 11 closes the first finite line-incidence regime
   `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=r-t=2`. The quotient has the closed form
   `Q_S=C(X-s_T)+C1`, bad-line landing is one conic `det(s_T,p_T)=0`, and
   the conditional slope count is `C2=O(n)` with generic `C2<=4`. This is a
   BANKABLE_LEMMA / AUDIT only for the low regime, not a proof of `conj:B`.
12. Cycle 12 gives the `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=r-t=3` quotient/quadric
    calculation. `Q_S` depends on `e1(T),e2(T)` and not `e3(T)`, while
    bad-line landing is a quadric in `(e1,e2,e3)` with leading coefficient
    `wedge([W]_E,[Bnum]_E)`. It does not prove a `C2` bound; the next wall is
    slope-fiber collapse on the split-cubic locus.
13. Cycle 13 proves the base-component complete-intersection route off
    resonance: `Delta=Res(L_T,E)*([I_S]_E wedge [Bnum]_E)`, the resultant is
    nonzero on valid co-supports, and after splitting
    `Delta=Delta_0+alpha Delta_1`, the two base quadrics are coprime off
    `R0 union Ra union Rb`. Therefore `C2=O(p)=O(n)` for `D=F_p`,
    `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, away from the classified resonance strata. The
    remaining target is the resonance locus where the base quadrics share a
    surface component.
14. Cycle 14 shows the resonance strata are not excluded by the currently
    banked source hypotheses and reduces the residual problem to the explicit
    slope equations
    `q1 z^2-(p1-q2)z-p2=0`, `tau_3=p1-zq1 in B` on a base surface
    `Sigma subset B^3`. The live wall is to prove `O(p)` slope image on
    every source-valid resonance surface or construct a source-valid
    `Theta(p^2)` slope counterpacket.
15. Cycle 15 sharpens that surface slope problem to the affine equation
    `L_z(tau)=iota-z mu=0` in `A=F[X]/E`. The `B`-linear columns
    `c1(z),c2(z),c3(z)` and determinant consistency polynomial
    `Q(z_0,z_1)=det[c1|c2|c3|c0]` are now the live invariant. Rank `3` alone
    is not enough: rank `3` with `Q!=0` gives a curve-sized slope set, while
    rank `3` with `Q==0` identically is the possible `Theta(q_line)` regime.
16. Cycle 16 banks the safe side: off `R0`, if `Q` is not identically zero,
    then `C2<=4p=O(p)=O(n)` in the `D=F_p`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3` test regime.
    It sharpens the residual wall to the `Q==0` branch restricted to distinct
    `D`-split cubics. The proposed trace/Gram criterion for `Q==0` is useful
    but remains AUDIT until checked by a local symbolic/finite verifier.

17. Cycle 17 attempted a rank-det split scanner but produced no clean
    `response.md` and no `output_files/` deliverable. It remains AUDIT only.
18. Cycle 18 banks monicity of the `tau_3` determinant in the
    `{[W]_E,b}` basis and sharpens the residual branch to resonance
    slope-map collapse.
19. Cycle 19 proposes rank-one/gate formulas but is AUDIT only.
20. Cycle 20 banks the rank-one leading coefficient, quadric-branch normal
    form, and the `D` gate `det M=(c_b/kappa^2)D`.
21. Cycle 21 banks differential collapse gates: simultaneous branch collapse
    is governed by `J_A=J_Aprime=0`, and the resultant of those gates is the
    Cycle 20 gate `D`. It does not bank the stronger independence claim.
22. Cycle 22 banks the decoupling identity
    `Im_alpha(J_A)=2 Im_alpha(d)+Im_alpha(c) f_2` on `Delta1==0`. Therefore,
    on the explicit stratum `c in B`, `d notin B`, `Delta1==0`, `D=0`,
    alignment is impossible in odd characteristic if that stratum is
    source-valid and nonempty. It does not bank nonemptiness or a
    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
23. Cycle 23 proves the Cycle 22 `c in B`, `d notin B` nonemptiness wall is
    empty. In that branch `ell=[X^p-X]_E=mu*(xi+c/2)` and
    `D=-mu^2(c^2/4-d)kappa`, so `D!=0` off `R0`. The only surviving separated
    `D=0` lane is now `c notin B`.
24. Cycle 24 proves the norm factorization
    `D=N(ell)kappa`, with
    `ell=[X^p-X]_E=mu*(xi+c/2)+delta_c` and
    `N(ell)=prod_{a in F_p}E(a)`. Since source-valid denominators are nonzero
    on `D=F_p`, `N(ell)!=0`; off `R0`, `kappa!=0`; hence the entire
    source-valid `D=0`, off-`R0` branch is empty in this restricted
    `t=2,j=3` calculation. The live branch returns to the non-`D=0`
    determinant-split `Q==0` wall.
25. Cycle 25 banks the six-term Plucker/Laplace expansion of the
    slope-fiber determinant `Q(z)` and confirms that the `z`-free determinant
    gate `det M=(c_b/kappa^2)D` is separate from `Q(z)`. It also cuts the
    false route `Q(z)=0 iff realized slope`: `Q(z)=0` is sufficient only when
    the coefficient columns `C(z)=[c_1(z)|c_2(z)|c_3(z)]` have rank `3`; on
    rank-drop strata the augmented-rank minors of `[C(z)|c_0(z)]` are required.
26. Cycle 26 banks the DEP/NONDEP rank dichotomy. In NONDEP, rank-drop costs
    only `O(p)` slopes; away from rank-drop, `Q(z)=0` is necessary and
    sufficient for affine `tau in B^3` consistency. The `c notin B`,
    `c_b != 0` branch is NONDEP by leading-symbol independence. The proposed
    top-degree `Q_4` obstruction is a high-value next target but remains
    audit-only until independently verified, and the distinct split-cubic gate
    remains separate from affine consistency.
27. Cycle 27 identifies a candidate corrected top coefficient
    `Q_4=N(c_b)*(Im(d)^2-Im(c)*Im(conj(c)d))`. For `c notin B`, it factors
    as `N(c_b)*Im(c)^2*E(-Im(d)/Im(c))`, so `Q_4=0` iff `E` has an `F_p`
    root iff the Cycle 24 locator norm `prod_{a in F_p}E(a)` vanishes.
    For `c in B`, it becomes `N(c_b)*Im(d)^2`, nonzero under separatedness.
    This appears to cut the restricted source-valid `Q==0` branch, but it is
    banked only as `BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / AUDIT` until Cycle 28
    independently rederives the column algebra.
28. Cycle 28 independently confirms the `q1/q2` closed forms from the Cycle 20
    wedge identities and verifies the `Q_4` formula. The restricted
    `D=F_p`, `B=F_p`, `F=F_{p^2}`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, off-`R0`,
    `c_b!=0`, source-valid separated branch is now closed as a local
    `PROOF / ROUTE_CUT / AUDIT`: `Q` is never identically zero, so Cycle 16
    gives `C2<=4p=O(p)`. This is not a corrected-reserve, MCA, protocol, or
    prize theorem.
29. Cycle 29 shows that the Cycle 28 determinant mechanism changes meaning at
    `t=2,j=4`. The quotient depends on `tau_1,tau_2,tau_3`, while the
    locator residue introduces `tau_4`, giving a square affine system
    `M(z)tau=-C_0(z)` in the four-dimensional `B`-space `A=F[X]/E`. The top
    symbol persists as `TopSym(det_B M(z))=-N(kappa)N(z)^2Q_4`, with the
    same source-valid nonzero Cycle 28 locator quantity, but this only proves
    generic invertibility. It does not imply an `O(p)` slope bound for `j=4`.
    The live wall is the split-quartic gate for the rational preimage
    `tau(z)`.
30. Cycle 30 reduces the `t=2,j=4` split-quartic gate to the explicit
    one-`F`-quadric / two-`B`-quadric equation
    `Phi(tau)=iota wedge_F mu=0`, equivalently
    `kappa*N(lambda)-(ell[Q_S]_E) wedge_F (b lambda)=0`, with
    `lambda=[L_T]_E`. This banks the gate reduction only. Codex direct
    finite scans for random small source-valid instances lean toward
    `O(p)`-scale realized slope counts (`p=29` average about `1.08p`, not a
    positive-density fraction of `p^2`). The live wall is now to explain this
    split-locus collapse or construct a genuine source-valid `Theta(q_line)`
    growing-prime family.
31. Cycle 31 challenges the Cycle 30 scan interpretation and proposes the
    quartic monodromy invariant as the right object. Codex audit does not bank
    the claim that `O(p)` collapse is impossible and does not bank a
    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket. It does bank the next exact wall:
    compute/source-audit `disc_X L_{tau(z)}`, the resolvent cubic, the correct
    base-field model for `z in F_{p^2}` with splitting over `B=F_p`, and the
    geometric monodromy group.
32. Cycle 32 cuts the one-variable `B(z)` / `F(z)` framing. The correct model
    is a two-dimensional `B`-surface over `A^2_B`, with
    `z=z_0+alpha z_1`. Codex added a local Cramer-system factorization
    histogram checker. The checker matches direct support enumeration away
    from the determinant curve `Delta=0`; discrepancies are recorded as
    `singular_split_C2`. This is `ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL /
    AUDIT`, not proof of `S_4`, not proof of positive density, and not a
    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
33. Cycle 33 banks the singular determinant curve bound. Under the Cycle 29
    top-symbol nonzero hypotheses, `Delta(z_0,z_1)=det_B M(z)` has degree at
    most four and is not identically zero, so `Delta=0` contributes at most
    `4p=O(p)` split slopes. This is a restricted `BANKABLE_LEMMA /
    CONDITIONAL / AUDIT`, not a proof of off-curve positive density or a
    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
34. Cycle 34 banks the off-curve dominance lemma. On the source-valid dense
    open of the restricted `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` branch, the rational map
    `psi:z |-> tau(z)=M(z)^(-1)(-C_0(z))` has generic `B`-Jacobian rank two
    and is birational onto the Cycle 30 quadric image. This cuts the hidden
    rank-one / curve-collapse route for `O(p)` off-curve counts. It does not
    prove geometric `S_4`, positive split density, arithmetic/geometric
    monodromy equality, or a `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket. The live wall is
    the geometric monodromy and constant-field test.
35. Cycle 35 banks a finite-place S4 certificate. For any fixed source-valid
    restricted `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` instance, off-`Delta` squarefree
    factorization types `"4"` and `"13"` certify `G_arith=S_4`, and the even
    type `"13"` cuts the sign constant-field obstruction, giving
    `G_geom=G_arith=S_4` for that instance. This turns the Cycle 32 finite
    histogram into serious `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence, but it is not a
    uniform-in-`p` theorem and not a `COUNTERPACKET`. The live wall is now to
    construct an explicit source-valid growing-prime family with uniform S4,
    or identify the exact obstruction.
36. Cycle 36 banks an explicit source-valid growing-prime denominator family
    for primes `p = 3 mod 4`: `B=F_p`, `F=F_{p^2}=B(alpha)`,
    `alpha^2=-1`, `E=X^2+alpha X+1`, `b=X`. It reduces uniform geometric S4
    to a single-prime finite certificate after good-reduction monotonicity.
    Codex corrected the raw shortcut: quartic transitivity/geometric
    irreducibility must be certified in addition to resolvent irreducibility
    and discriminant nonsquareness. This is not a COUNTERPACKET and not an
    MCA/protocol/prize theorem.
37. Cycle 37 re-checks the source-valid hand gates for the Cycle 36 explicit
    family: `E(a)=(a^2+1)+alpha*a` has no `F_p` root, `E` and `E^tau` are
    separated on `D=F_p`, `c=alpha` gives the `c notin B`, `c_b!=0` branch,
    and choosing `u=1+X` gives `kappa=1`. With `c=alpha`, `d=1`, the top
    symbol quantity remains `Q4=N(c_b)!=0`, so `Delta=0` contributes at most
    `4p` slopes in this restricted branch. Cycle 37 did not produce a working
    single-prime S4 certificate: the supplied Python checker is type-malformed
    and fails by mixing `F`-elements with residue pairs. Status:
    `BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT`, not `PROOF`
    and not `COUNTERPACKET`.
38. Cycle 38 identifies and repairs the exact Cycle 37 checker type error.
    The repaired free data are actual `F`-elements
    `W_{n-1..n-4}=1,alpha,1+alpha,1`, not residue-pairs. Codex ran the
    patched checker locally at `p=31`; it returns
    `PASS_S4_finite_place=true` with factorization witnesses `"4"` and
    `"13"`, a nonsquare-discriminant witness, and no singular points on the
    tested line. This is finite-place `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence plus a
    bankable checker repair. It is not a uniform theorem, not geometric `S_4`
    over `B(z_0,z_1)`, not a `COUNTERPACKET`, and not an MCA/protocol/prize
    theorem.
39. Cycle 39 proves the locator-collapse lemma in the explicit `t=2,j=4`
    family: `[X^p-X]_E=alpha` when `(-5/p)=+1`, and
    `[X^p-X]_E=-2X` when `(-5/p)=-1`. Thus the family splits into two fixed
    congruence subcases instead of varying freely with `p`. The `p=31`
    certificate covers only Subcase B; Subcase A and good reduction remain
    open.

19	counterpacket. It banks the exact inequality structure separating the distinct
20	slope image from total landings and cuts the attempted L2 route for safe-side
21	upper bounds.
22	
23	## Verdict
24	
25	Cycle51 is significant route hygiene.
26	
27	It clarifies that the MCA numerator is the distinct slope image
28	
29	```text
30	Z(u,v) = #{z : nu(z) >= 1},
31	```
32	
33	not the landing count
34	
35	```text
36	R(u,v) = sum_z nu(z).
37	```
38	
39	Bounding `R` is sufficient for MCA but stronger than needed. More importantly,
40	the usual L2 anticollision machinery goes in the wrong direction for an upper
41	bound on `Z`.
42

44	
45	For

48	nu(z) = #{T : z(T)=z},
49	R = sum_z nu(z),
50	Z = #{z : nu(z)>=1},
51	M2 = sum_z nu(z)^2,
52	```
53	
54	one has the sharp inequalities
55	
56	```text
57	Z <= R,
58	Z >= R^2/M2,
59	Z >= R/max_z nu(z),
60	M2 <= R max_z nu(z),
61	R-Z = sum_z (nu(z)-1)_+.
62	```
63	
64	Consequences:

67	  bound `Z <= R_line+O(j)`;
68	- an L2 upper bound on `M2` gives a lower bound on `Z`, not an upper bound;
69	- high landing multiplicity with small image is not a counterpacket to the
70	  distinct-slope bound.

78	Z_tan = O(j)
79	```
80	
81	by the earlier fixed-core/tangent packet bounds. The moving-scroll part is the
82	true unresolved piece:
83	
84	```text

86	```
87	
88	The finite random-incidence baseline remains
89	
90	```text
91	R_line = ceil(binomial(n,k+t)/Q^(t-1)).
92	```
93	
94	This is an averaged landing scale, not a deterministic per-line value-set
95	bound.
96	
97	## Route Cut
98	
99	Do not try to prove the safe-side value-set upper theorem using only an L2
100	anticollision estimate.
101	
102	Cauchy-Schwarz gives
103	
104	```text
105	Z >= R^2/M2.
106	```

109	Cycle44/Cycle47 lower/failure branch, where the goal is to show many slopes.
110	It is not a proof route for the safe-side upper branch, where the goal is to
111	show few slopes.
112	
113	The upper branch needs either:

115	1. the stronger landing upper bound `R <= R_line+O(j)`; or
116	2. a direct value-set/splitting-density theorem for `Z`.

121	function `nu`.

124	  `Z >= R^2/M2`.
125	- Cycle51 needs an upper bound on support size:
126	  `Z <= R_line+O(j)`.
127	
128	So the L2 anticollision theorem cannot be recycled as the safe-side upper
129	theorem.

135	```
136

138	
139	> For a transverse balanced, quotient-action-rank-separated Hankel pencil,
140	> bound the number of slopes whose moving fiber contains a fully split
141	> squarefree locator:

# Cycle 51 Balanced Scroll Value-Set Audit

Status: `BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / AUDIT`.

Model/run:

- Model: `claude-opus-4-8`
- Run: `2026-06-19T09-43-26-682Z-cycle51-balanced-scroll-valueset-vs-landing-fc07aeb3`
- Preserved raw artifacts:
  `raw/cycle51_balanced_scroll_valueset_vs_landing/response.md`,
  `raw/cycle51_balanced_scroll_valueset_vs_landing/raw_response.jsonl`,
  `raw/cycle51_balanced_scroll_valueset_vs_landing/raw_response.json`,
  `raw/cycle51_balanced_scroll_valueset_vs_landing/run_result.json`,
  `raw/cycle51_balanced_scroll_valueset_vs_landing/input_manifest.json`,
  and `raw/cycle51_balanced_scroll_valueset_vs_landing/prompt_sent.md`.
- `output_files/` was empty in the source run.

The answer does **not** prove the value-set bound and does **not** give a
counterpacket. It banks the exact inequality structure separating the distinct
slope image from total landings and cuts the attempted L2 route for safe-side
upper bounds.

## Verdict

Cycle51 is significant route hygiene.

It clarifies that the MCA numerator is the distinct slope image

```text
Z(u,v) = #{z : nu(z) >= 1},
```

not the landing count

```text
R(u,v) = sum_z nu(z).
```

Bounding `R` is sufficient for MCA but stronger than needed. More importantly,
the usual L2 anticollision machinery goes in the wrong direction for an upper
bound on `Z`.

## Bankable Lemma: Moment Sandwich

For

```text
nu(z) = #{T : z(T)=z},
R = sum_z nu(z),
Z = #{z : nu(z)>=1},
M2 = sum_z nu(z)^2,
```

one has the sharp inequalities

```text
Z <= R,
Z >= R^2/M2,
Z >= R/max_z nu(z),
M2 <= R max_z nu(z),
R-Z = sum_z (nu(z)-1)_+.
```

Consequences:

- a landing-count upper bound `R <= R_line+O(j)` implies the MCA numerator
  bound `Z <= R_line+O(j)`;
- an L2 upper bound on `M2` gives a lower bound on `Z`, not an upper bound;
- high landing multiplicity with small image is not a counterpacket to the
  distinct-slope bound.

## Bankable Decomposition

The `O(j)` term belongs to the tangent/core/common-envelope part:

```text
Z = Z_tan + Z_mov,
Z_tan = O(j)
```

by the earlier fixed-core/tangent packet bounds. The moving-scroll part is the
true unresolved piece:

```text
Z_mov = #{z : M_z meets Proj_V(Split_L^sf)}.
```

The finite random-incidence baseline remains

```text
R_line = ceil(binomial(n,k+t)/Q^(t-1)).
```

This is an averaged landing scale, not a deterministic per-line value-set
bound.

## Route Cut

Do not try to prove the safe-side value-set upper theorem using only an L2
anticollision estimate.

Cauchy-Schwarz gives

```text
Z >= R^2/M2.
```

Thus small `M2` pushes `Z` upward. It is the correct direction for the
Cycle44/Cycle47 lower/failure branch, where the goal is to show many slopes.
It is not a proof route for the safe-side upper branch, where the goal is to
show few slopes.

The upper branch needs either:

1. the stronger landing upper bound `R <= R_line+O(j)`; or
2. a direct value-set/splitting-density theorem for `Z`.

## Relation To Cycle44

In the matched residue ledger, Cycle44 and Cycle51 study the same multiplicity
function `nu`.

- Cycle44 uses `M2` to prove a lower bound on support size:
  `Z >= R^2/M2`.
- Cycle51 needs an upper bound on support size:
  `Z <= R_line+O(j)`.

So the L2 anticollision theorem cannot be recycled as the safe-side upper
theorem.

## Exact New Wall

```text
W-MCA-BALANCED-SCROLL-VALUESET-MONODROMY-DENSITY
```

Direct value-set version:

> For a transverse balanced, quotient-action-rank-separated Hankel pencil,
> bound the number of slopes whose moving fiber contains a fully split
> squarefree locator:
>
> ```text
> Z_mov = #{z : M_z cap Proj_V(Split_L^sf) != empty}
>        <= R_line + O(j).
> ```

The proposed route is monodromy/splitting-density, not L2:

```text
Z_mov = Q * delta_split(P) + error,
delta_split(P) <= 1/j + o(1)
```

after quotient and tangent templates are removed.

## Next Prompt Target

```text
W-MCA-BALANCED-SCROLL-TOTALLY-SPLIT-DENSITY
```

Ask for a proof or counterpacket to the density-decay claim:

```text
For a balanced quotient-separated moving scroll, the density of slopes whose
fiber contains a fully L-split squarefree locator is <= 1/j+o(1).
```

A counterpacket must have many distinct slopes and low fiber multiplicity. A
large landing count concentrated on few slopes is not sufficient.

1	# Cycle 50 Reduced Moving-Scroll Balanced-Index Audit
2	
3	Status: `BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / AUDIT`.
4	
5	Model/run:
6	
7	- Model: `claude-opus-4-8`
8	- Run: `2026-06-19T09-06-20-338Z-cycle50-reduced-moving-scroll-balanced-index-b896e1af`
9	- Preserved raw artifacts:
10	  `raw/cycle50_reduced_moving_scroll_balanced_index/response.md`,
11	  `raw/cycle50_reduced_moving_scroll_balanced_index/raw_response.jsonl`,
12	  `raw/cycle50_reduced_moving_scroll_balanced_index/raw_response.json`,
13	  `raw/cycle50_reduced_moving_scroll_balanced_index/run_result.json`,
14	  `raw/cycle50_reduced_moving_scroll_balanced_index/input_manifest.json`,
15	  and `raw/cycle50_reduced_moving_scroll_balanced_index/prompt_sent.md`.
16	- `output_files/` was empty in the source run.
17	
18	The answer explicitly does **not** claim `PROOF` and does **not** give a
19	`COUNTERPACKET`. It banks a useful rank-one/slope-map reduction and cuts the
20	idea that balanced minimal indices automatically prove the desired
21	deterministic line bound.
22	
23	## Verdict
24	
25	Cycle50 is positive progress, but it does not solve the balanced-index wall.
26	
27	The most useful contribution is a sharper identification of what the reduced
28	moving-scroll incidence count actually is:
29	
30	```text
31	split locator T -> (a(T),b(T)) = (H(u)ell_T, H(v)ell_T) in F^t x F^t.
32	```
33	
34	Transverse slopes are the value set of the partial map determined by the
35	rank-one condition
36	
37	```text
38	a(T)+z b(T)=0,  b(T) != 0.
39	```
40	
41	This cleanly separates:
42	
43	- total landings / rank-one split locators;
44	- distinct slope image;
45	- tangent/common-envelope core;
46	- quotient-aligned moving-scroll concentration.
47	
48	## Bankable Lemma A: Rank-One Slope Fibration
49	
50	For a fixed Hankel pencil `H(u)+zH(v)` and a split locator `ell_T`, define
51	
52	```text
53	a(T)=H(u)ell_T,
54	b(T)=H(v)ell_T.
55	```
56	
57	Then a transverse locator realizes a slope iff
58	
59	```text
60	b(T) != 0
61	and
62	rank[a(T) | b(T)] <= 1.
63	```
64	
65	When this holds, the realized slope is unique:
66	
67	```text
68	z(T) = -a_m(T)/b_m(T)
69	```
70	
71	for any coordinate `m` with `b_m(T) != 0`.
72	
73	Therefore the distinct slope count is the image size of a partial map from
74	split locators to `F`, and it is bounded by the number of rank-one transverse
75	split locators.
76	
77	## Bankable Lemma B: Disjoint Moving Fibers
78	
79	Let
80	
81	```text
82	K0 = ker H(u) cap ker H(v),
83	L_z = ker(H(u)+zH(v)),
84	M_z = L_z/K0.
85	```
86	
87	For `z != z'`,
88	
89	```text
90	M_z cap M_z' = 0.
91	```
92	
93	Indeed, if a vector lies in both kernels, subtracting the two equations gives
94	`H(v)x=0`, hence also `H(u)x=0`, so `x in K0`.
95	
96	This supports the Cycle49 view that, after quotienting by the core, a
97	transverse projected locator belongs to at most one moving fiber.
98	
99	## Bankable Lemma C: `R_line` Is the Averaged Landing Baseline
100	
101	The rank-one locus in `F^t x F^t` has codimension `t-1`, so for
102	
103	```text
104	N = binomial(n,k+t) = binomial(n,j),
105	Q = |F|,
106	```
107	
108	the expected total landing count under random-anchor averaging is
109	
110	```text
111	N / Q^(t-1).
112	```
113	
114	This is the finite correction already banked as
115	
116	```text
117	R_line = ceil(N / Q^(t-1)).
118	```
119	
120	Thus `R_line` is the correct finite random-incidence baseline. A bare
121	`n^{1+o(1)}` term immediately above the entropy equality is not the correct
122	finite statement.
123	
124	## Route Cut 1: Balanced Index Is Not Enough
125	
126	Balanced right minimal indices remove the extreme unbalanced-directrix
127	quotient-component packets, especially the `d_M(E)=1` punctured-fiber
128	coequalizer shape. They do **not** by themselves exclude all quotient or
129	bounded-defect quotient-action packets.
130	
131	The separate quotient-action-rank hypothesis remains load-bearing:
132	
133	```text
134	d_M(E)=deg minpoly([X^M]_E)
135	```
136	
137	and its moving-scroll analogue still have to be controlled.
138	
139	## Route Cut 2: Average Does Not Give Worst-Case Upper
140	
141	Cycle47 gives an averaged random-anchor estimate. Cycle50 correctly observes
142	that `R_line` is the averaged landing scale, but this does not prove a
143	deterministic per-line upper bound for every balanced quotient-separated
144	pencil.
145	
146	The missing theorem is a worst-case inverse theorem, not another expectation
147	identity.
148	
149	## Audit Correction: Image vs Landing Count
150	
151	Cycle50 sometimes treats the distinct slope image bound as equivalent to a
152	landing-count bound. That equivalence is too strong.
153	
154	Let
155	
156	```text
157	R(u,v) = #{T : rank[a(T)|b(T)] <= 1, b(T)!=0}
158	Z(u,v) = #{z : exists T with z(T)=z}.
159	```
160	
161	Then
162	
163	```text
164	Z(u,v) <= R(u,v).
165	```
166	
167	So a first-moment landing bound
168	
169	```text
170	R(u,v) <= R_line + (j+1)
171	```
172	
173	would imply the desired distinct-slope bound, but the reverse need not hold.
174	A high-multiplicity collapse can make `R(u,v)` huge while `Z(u,v)` is small.
175	
176	Therefore the exact next wall should not be phrased only as Cycle44 landing
177	first moment unless we explicitly choose the stronger sufficient theorem.
178	
179	Likewise, a pure `L2` bound gives useful anticollision information but does not
180	by itself upper-bound the distinct slope image unless it is coupled with an
181	upper bound for total landings. Cauchy-Schwarz usually gives a lower bound on
182	image size from `L^2/M2`, not an upper bound.
183	
184	## Exact New Wall
185	
186	The wall exposed by Cycle50 is:
187	
188	```text
189	W-MCA-BALANCED-SCROLL-CONFIG-EQUIDISTRIBUTION
190	```
191	
192	Precise version:
193	
194	> For a fixed transverse Hankel pencil with balanced right minimal indices and
195	> no quotient-action-rank defect, bound the value set
196	>
197	> ```text
198	> Z(u,v) = #{ z(T) : T split, b(T)!=0, rank[a(T)|b(T)]<=1 }
199	> ```
200	>
201	> by `R_line + O(j)` or produce a source-valid counterpacket.
202	
203	A stronger sufficient theorem is:
204	
205	```text
206	R(u,v) <= R_line + O(j).
207	```
208	
209	But this stronger landing-count statement should not be confused with the
210	actual MCA numerator, which is the distinct slope image `Z(u,v)`.
211	
212	## Next Prompt Target
213	
214	The next round should force this distinction:
215	
216	```text
217	W-MCA-BALANCED-SCROLL-VALUESET-VS-LANDING
218	```
219	
220	Ask for either:
221	
222	1. a proof that the distinct value set `Z(u,v)` is bounded by
223	   `R_line + O(j)` under balanced-index and quotient-separated hypotheses;
224	2. a proof of the stronger landing bound `R(u,v) <= R_line + O(j)`;
225	3. or a counterpacket where balanced-index quotient-separated pencils have
226	   `Z(u,v) > R_line + O(j)`.
227	
228	The counterpacket must exceed the distinct **slope image**, not merely total
229	landing multiplicity.
230

The answer explicitly does **not** claim `PROOF` and does **not** give a
`COUNTERPACKET`. It banks a useful rank-one/slope-map reduction and cuts the
idea that balanced minimal indices automatically prove the desired
deterministic line bound.

## Verdict

Cycle50 is positive progress, but it does not solve the balanced-index wall.

The most useful contribution is a sharper identification of what the reduced
moving-scroll incidence count actually is:

```text
split locator T -> (a(T),b(T)) = (H(u)ell_T, H(v)ell_T) in F^t x F^t.
```

Transverse slopes are the value set of the partial map determined by the
rank-one condition

```text
a(T)+z b(T)=0,  b(T) != 0.
```

This cleanly separates:

- total landings / rank-one split locators;
- distinct slope image;
- tangent/common-envelope core;
- quotient-aligned moving-scroll concentration.

## Bankable Lemma A: Rank-One Slope Fibration

For a fixed Hankel pencil `H(u)+zH(v)` and a split locator `ell_T`, define

```text
a(T)=H(u)ell_T,
b(T)=H(v)ell_T.
```

Then a transverse locator realizes a slope iff

```text
b(T) != 0
and
rank[a(T) | b(T)] <= 1.
```

When this holds, the realized slope is unique:

```text
z(T) = -a_m(T)/b_m(T)
```

for any coordinate `m` with `b_m(T) != 0`.

Therefore the distinct slope count is the image size of a partial map from
split locators to `F`, and it is bounded by the number of rank-one transverse
split locators.

## Bankable Lemma B: Disjoint Moving Fibers

Let

```text
K0 = ker H(u) cap ker H(v),
L_z = ker(H(u)+zH(v)),
M_z = L_z/K0.
```

For `z != z'`,

```text
M_z cap M_z' = 0.
```

Indeed, if a vector lies in both kernels, subtracting the two equations gives
`H(v)x=0`, hence also `H(u)x=0`, so `x in K0`.

This supports the Cycle49 view that, after quotienting by the core, a
transverse projected locator belongs to at most one moving fiber.

## Bankable Lemma C: `R_line` Is the Averaged Landing Baseline

The rank-one locus in `F^t x F^t` has codimension `t-1`, so for

```text
N = binomial(n,k+t) = binomial(n,j),
Q = |F|,
```

the expected total landing count under random-anchor averaging is

```text
N / Q^(t-1).
```

This is the finite correction already banked as

```text
R_line = ceil(N / Q^(t-1)).
```

Thus `R_line` is the correct finite random-incidence baseline. A bare
`n^{1+o(1)}` term immediately above the entropy equality is not the correct
finite statement.

## Route Cut 1: Balanced Index Is Not Enough

Balanced right minimal indices remove the extreme unbalanced-directrix
quotient-component packets, especially the `d_M(E)=1` punctured-fiber
coequalizer shape. They do **not** by themselves exclude all quotient or
bounded-defect quotient-action packets.

The separate quotient-action-rank hypothesis remains load-bearing:

```text
d_M(E)=deg minpoly([X^M]_E)
```

and its moving-scroll analogue still have to be controlled.

## Route Cut 2: Average Does Not Give Worst-Case Upper

Cycle47 gives an averaged random-anchor estimate. Cycle50 correctly observes
that `R_line` is the averaged landing scale, but this does not prove a
deterministic per-line upper bound for every balanced quotient-separated
pencil.

The missing theorem is a worst-case inverse theorem, not another expectation
identity.

## Audit Correction: Image vs Landing Count

Cycle50 sometimes treats the distinct slope image bound as equivalent to a
landing-count bound. That equivalence is too strong.

Let

```text
R(u,v) = #{T : rank[a(T)|b(T)] <= 1, b(T)!=0}
Z(u,v) = #{z : exists T with z(T)=z}.
```

Then

```text
Z(u,v) <= R(u,v).
```

So a first-moment landing bound

```text
R(u,v) <= R_line + (j+1)
```

would imply the desired distinct-slope bound, but the reverse need not hold.
A high-multiplicity collapse can make `R(u,v)` huge while `Z(u,v)` is small.

Therefore the exact next wall should not be phrased only as Cycle44 landing
first moment unless we explicitly choose the stronger sufficient theorem.

Likewise, a pure `L2` bound gives useful anticollision information but does not
by itself upper-bound the distinct slope image unless it is coupled with an
upper bound for total landings. Cauchy-Schwarz usually gives a lower bound on
image size from `L^2/M2`, not an upper bound.

## Exact New Wall

The wall exposed by Cycle50 is:

```text
W-MCA-BALANCED-SCROLL-CONFIG-EQUIDISTRIBUTION
```

Precise version:

> For a fixed transverse Hankel pencil with balanced right minimal indices and
> no quotient-action-rank defect, bound the value set
>
> ```text
> Z(u,v) = #{ z(T) : T split, b(T)!=0, rank[a(T)|b(T)]<=1 }
> ```
>
> by `R_line + O(j)` or produce a source-valid counterpacket.

A stronger sufficient theorem is:

```text
R(u,v) <= R_line + O(j).
```

But this stronger landing-count statement should not be confused with the
actual MCA numerator, which is the distinct slope image `Z(u,v)`.

## Next Prompt Target

The next round should force this distinction:

```text
W-MCA-BALANCED-SCROLL-VALUESET-VS-LANDING
```

Ask for either:

1. a proof that the distinct value set `Z(u,v)` is bounded by
   `R_line + O(j)` under balanced-index and quotient-separated hypotheses;
2. a proof of the stronger landing bound `R(u,v) <= R_line + O(j)`;
3. or a counterpacket where balanced-index quotient-separated pencils have
   `Z(u,v) > R_line + O(j)`.

The counterpacket must exceed the distinct **slope image**, not merely total
landing multiplicity.

# Cycle 49 Syndrome Transverse-Secant Audit

Status: `BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / AUDIT`.

Model/run:

- Model: `claude-opus-4-8`
- Run: `2026-06-19T08-47-44-828Z-cycle49-syndrome-transverse-secant-inverse-7aeb3c2c`
- Preserved raw artifacts:
  `raw/cycle49_syndrome_transverse_secant_inverse/response.md`,
  `raw/cycle49_syndrome_transverse_secant_inverse/raw_response.jsonl`,
  `raw/cycle49_syndrome_transverse_secant_inverse/raw_response.json`,
  `raw/cycle49_syndrome_transverse_secant_inverse/run_result.json`,
  `raw/cycle49_syndrome_transverse_secant_inverse/input_manifest.json`,
  and `raw/cycle49_syndrome_transverse_secant_inverse/prompt_sent.md`.
- `output_files/` was empty in the source run.

The answer explicitly does **not** claim a proof of the positive upper theorem
and does **not** give a counterpacket. It banks an exact syndrome/Hankel normal
form, a Kronecker-pencil moving-core reduction, and a route cut for a tempting
but false low-degree-scroll shortcut.

## Verdict

Cycle49 is significant, but it is not a full solve.

The main upgrade is that the upper-side object is now much cleaner than the
residue-coordinate formulation. For a fixed Reed-Solomon code, every denominator
degree branch is represented by one syndrome-line incidence problem, and the
active inverse wall is compressed from the ambient syndrome dimension
`r = n-k` to a reduced moving space of dimension at most `2t`.

This is a strong organizing lemma for the safe-side MCA problem. It does not
prove that rich lines are only quotient/tangent templates.

## Bankable Lemma A: Hankel-Pencil Normal Form

Let `C = RS[F,L,k]`, `n=|L|`, `r=n-k`, and let `W_T` be the span of the parity
check columns indexed by a `j`-set `T`. Put

```text
t = r-j.
```

For a syndrome vector `w in F^r`, define the `t x (j+1)` Hankel matrix

```text
H(w)_{m,l} = w_{m+l},  0 <= m < t, 0 <= l <= j.
```

If `L_T(X)` is the monic degree-`j` complement locator and `ell_T` is its
coefficient vector, then

```text
u + z v in W_T  <=>  (H(u) + z H(v)) ell_T = 0.
```

Therefore the MCA numerator is exactly a problem of counting slopes `z` for
which the kernel of a Hankel pencil contains a squarefree fully-`L`-split monic
degree-`j` locator, with the transversality condition

```text
H(v) ell_T != 0.
```

This simultaneously covers the `t<sigma`, `t=sigma`, and `t>sigma` branches:
changing `j` changes `t=r-j`.

## Bankable Lemma B: Core/Moving Kronecker Reduction

For a line of syndromes `u+zv`, set

```text
K0 = ker H(u) cap ker H(v).
```

Then

```text
dim K0 >= j+1-2t.
```

For generic `z`, the kernel of `H(u)+zH(v)` decomposes as

```text
ker(H(u)+zH(v)) = K0 plus M_z,
```

with

```text
dim M_z <= t.
```

Thus the moving problem lives in

```text
V = F[X]_{<=j}/K0,   dim V <= 2t.
```

Kronecker pencil theory further gives right minimal indices
`eps_i` with

```text
sum_i eps_i <= t.
```

The moving kernels sweep a rational scroll of degree at most `t` in the reduced
space `P(V)`. This is the clean version of the prior locator-scroll/circuit
language: the common core is the contained/tangent part, while quotient
components appear as unbalanced minimal-index scrolls.

## Route Cut

Do **not** bank the shortcut:

```text
common core removal + scroll degree <= t => O(n) slopes.
```

The projected split-locator configuration is structured, not generic. A
low-degree scroll can still contain many projected split locators when it aligns
with a quotient structure such as `X^M`. This is exactly where the Cycle48
quotient-component and fixed-defect quotient-anchor packets live.

## Finite Accounting

Cycle49 preserves the Cycle48 finite correction. A safe-side finite theorem
cannot replace the random line-occupancy scale by a bare `n^{1+o(1)}` term.
The expected line scale is

```text
R_line = ceil(binomial(n,k+t) / Q^(t-1)).
```

A realistic finite upper shape is therefore

```text
Pi_j(C) <= U_quotient + R_line + A_theta(n),
```

where `U_quotient` charges quotient-component/fixed-defect packets and
`A_theta(n)` charges contained/tangent/common-envelope contributions.

## Exact New Wall

```text
W-MCA-REDUCED-MOVING-SCROLL-INCIDENCE
```

In the reduced space

```text
V = F[X]_{<=j}/K0,  dim V <= 2t,
```

with moving kernel `M_z` and Kronecker scroll of degree at most `t`, prove that
after removing unbalanced minimal-index quotient scrolls and the contained /
tangent core, the number of slopes satisfying

```text
M_z cap Proj_V(Split_L^sf) != empty
```

is at most

```text
R_line + A_theta(n).
```

## Next Prompt Target

The smallest clean next target is:

```text
W-MCA-REDUCED-MOVING-SCROLL-BALANCED-INDEX
```

Ask for either a proof or a counterpacket to:

```text
If the Hankel pencil H(u)+zH(v) has right minimal indices all within 1 of each
other, then

#{ z : M_z meets Proj_V(Split_L^sf) } <= R_line + (j+1).
```

This is the genuinely aperiodic balanced-index case. A proof here, combined
with the Cycle48 quotient-component classification, would move the positive
side much closer to matching the Cycle47 lower/failure branch. A counterpacket
here would be more serious than the Cycle48 quotient packets because it would
survive the current quotient/tangent explanations.

## Audit Note

Confidence is high for the linear-algebra reductions. Confidence is unknown for
the proposed inverse theorem. This result should be treated as a route
compression and wall sharpening, not as a prize-level proof.

1	I'll start by reading the file index, then the project files.I'll read the core project files first, then focus on the recent cycle material (49, 50, 51) and the Cycle44 anticollision wall.Now let me read the key recent cycle audits (49, 50) and the Cycle 44 anticollision audit, plus the active walls.Let me read the Cycle 50 full response, the active walls, and the Cycle 51 prompt that was actually sent.Let me read the Cycle 48 audit (quotient packets), Cycle 47 (variance/Bessel), the Cycle 44 response, and BANKED_LEMMAS to be precise about the L2 structure and the quotient hypotheses.I have the full picture: the Cycle 49 Hankel/scroll normal form, the Cycle 50 audit that split landing `R` from image `Z`, the Cycle 47 Bessel second-moment machinery, the Cycle 48 quotient-packet counterpacket, and the Cycle 44 cosupport L2-anticollision wall. Note this harness exposes only `Read` (no file-creation or execution tools), so — exactly as the Cycle 50 worker did — I deliver inline, with the checker as a runnable block for the director.
2	
3	---
4	
5	# Cycle 51 — Balanced Scroll Value Set vs Landing Count
6	
7	Status: `BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL`. Not `PROOF` of either bound, not `COUNTERPACKET`.
8	
9	Confidence: high on the moment geometry (elementary), high on the decomposition and the direction-of-L2 route-cut, high on the Cycle 44 duality. The residual per-line bound stays open. Ledger kept separate: `C=RS[F,L,k]`, syndromes over `F`, `Q=|F|`; in the matched quadratic instantiation `Q=q_line=p^2`, `B=F_p`, `q_gen=p` untouched.
10	
11	## 0. One-line verdict
12	
13	`Z(u,v) <= R_line + O(j)` is, as a formal statement, strictly weaker than the landing bound `R(u,v) <= R_line + O(j)`. But **the weakening buys nothing with the tools currently in hand**, because every moment method (first moment, L2) bounds `R` or `M_2`, and the only elementary upper route to the image is `Z <= R`. The value-set version becomes genuinely easier than the landing version *only* if one switches to a direct image-count (monodromy / totally-split-density) method that does not pass through `R`. So this cycle reduces `W-MCA-BALANCED-SCROLL-VALUESET-VS-LANDING` to two named pieces and pins down exactly where the easier-ness can live.
14	
15	## 1. Objects and the exact moment geometry
16	
17	Fix the transverse Hankel pencil `P(z)=H(u)+zH(v)` (`t x (j+1)`), `t=r-j`. For a split locator `T` (monic squarefree fully-`L`-split, `|T|=j`), `a(T)=H(u)ell_T`, `b(T)=H(v)ell_T`. By Cycle 50 Lemma A a transverse `T` (`b(T)!=0`) realizes a *unique* slope `z(T)=-a(T)_m/b(T)_m`, so `z(.)` is a partial function on split locators. Define the fiber-multiplicity function on slopes `z in F`:
18	
19	```text
20	nu(z) = #{ T split : b(T)!=0, rank[a(T)|b(T)]<=1, z(T)=z }.
21	R(u,v) = sum_z nu(z)        (total landings, L1 norm of nu)
22	Z(u,v) = #{ z : nu(z)>=1 }  (distinct slope image = MCA numerator, |supp nu|)
23	M_2     = sum_z nu(z)^2     (L2 norm squared)
24	```
25	
26	### BANKABLE_LEMMA 51.A (moment sandwich; the only inequalities available)
27	
28	```text
29	(i)   Z <= R                       [1[nu>=1] <= nu pointwise; equality iff nu in {0,1}]
30	(ii)  Z >= R^2 / M_2               [Cauchy-Schwarz: R = sum_{nu>0} nu*1 <= M_2^{1/2} Z^{1/2}]
31	(iii) Z >= R / max_z nu(z)         [R = sum nu <= Z * max nu]
32	(iv)  M_2 <= R * max_z nu(z)
33	(v)   R - Z = sum_z (nu(z)-1)_+    [the excess; this is the entire gap]
34	```
35	
36	These are sharp: when `nu` is flat (all positive values equal) (i)-(iii) are equalities. This lemma is the spine of everything below.
37	
38	The decisive structural fact: **`Z` is sandwiched as `R^2/M_2 <= Z <= R`, and BOTH walls of the sandwich are controlled by `R` (and `M_2`), never by an independent handle on the image.** There is no elementary inequality that upper-bounds `Z` from `M_2`.
39	
40	## 2. Where `R_line` and `O(j)` come from: core/transverse split
41	
42	By Cycle 50 Lemma B the moving fibers `M_z = ker P(z)/K0` are pairwise trivial-intersecting, so each transverse projected locator sits in exactly one `M_z`. Decompose the support of `nu` by the geometry of the realizing locators:
43	
44	```text
45	Z = Z_tan + Z_mov,
46	Z_tan = #{ realized z whose locators meet the bounded core/directrix template }
47	Z_mov = #{ realized z carried by generic rulings of the degree-<=t moving scroll }.
48	```
49	
50	`Z_tan` is the banked Cycle 48 template contribution: a fixed `k`-point tangent core, or a bounded-degree osculating/common-envelope directrix, contributes at most `(deg)*(j+1)` slopes. With degree `O(1)` after balanced reduction, `Z_tan = O(j)`. **This is exactly the `O(j)` term, and here the value-set framing is genuinely and unconditionally easier:** the template bounds the *image* `Z_tan <= j+1` directly, while the *landing* contribution of the same core is unbounded (a tangent core hosts arbitrarily many locators all landing on `<= j+1` slopes). So for the core, `R` is hopeless but `Z` is banked.
51	
52	`Z_mov` is the transverse scroll part, whose anchor-averaged scale is `R_line`:
53	
54	### BANKABLE_LEMMA 51.B (`R_line` is the averaged transverse landing, not a per-line image bound)
55	
56	With `lambda = N/Q^t`, `N = binom(n,k+t)=binom(n,j)`, Cycle 47's first moment `E_w nu_w(z)=lambda` summed over the `Q` slopes gives
57	
58	```text
59	sum_z E_w[nu_w(z)] = Q*lambda = N/Q^{t-1},
60	R_line = ceil(N/Q^{t-1}) = ceil( E_w[R(u,v)] ).
61	```
62	
63	So `R_line` is the anchor-*expectation* of the transverse landing count, hence (by `Z<=R`) an averaged ceiling for `Z_mov` only on average. The deterministic per-line statement is not implied (Cycle 50 Route Cut 2). The conjecture `Z <= R_line + O(j)` is therefore precisely `Z_mov <~ R_line` (open) plus `Z_tan = O(j)` (banked).
64	
65	## 3. ROUTE_CUT — L2 is the wrong direction; only `R` upper-bounds `Z`
66	
67	This answers the L2 question with full precision and is the central cut of the cycle.
68	
69	A per-line L2 / anticollision bound is a statement `M_2 <= B`. Feed it into Lemma 51.A: it can only enter through (ii) or (iv), and both yield
70	
71	```text
72	Z >= R^2 / M_2 >= R^2 / B        (a LOWER bound on Z).
73	```
74	
75	It is *consistent with* `Z = R` (flat `nu`, the equality case of (ii)). **Therefore an L2/anticollision bound gives no upper bound on `Z` whatsoever.** Concretely: small `M_2` means multiplicities are mostly `1`, which forces `Z` *up* toward `R`; it never forces `Z` down. The L2 estimate is a tool for showing the image is *large*, not small.
76	
77	Consequence: to prove `Z <= R_line + O(j)` one must either
78	
79	```text
80	(A) bound R(u,v) <= R_line + O(j)         (the stronger landing statement, Z<=R), or
81	(B) count the image |supp nu| directly    (a non-moment, structural/monodromy method).
82	```
83	
84	There is no third elementary route. In particular, **the value-set bound is not accessible by the first-and-second-moment program at all** unless that program is used to bound `R` (route A), in which case one has proved the stronger statement anyway. This is the exact sense in which "weaker statement" does not mean "easier proof" here.
85	
86	## 4. Are `Z`-bound and `R`-bound equivalent? (answers Q1, Q2)
87	
88	The gap is `R - Z = sum_z (nu(z)-1)_+` (51.A(v)). It is nonzero exactly when some fiber has `nu(z) >= 2`, i.e. when a single ruling `M_z` of the moving scroll carries `>= 2` split locators. So:
89	
90	- **They differ only through fiber multiplicity.** `R/max_z nu(z) <= Z <= R` (51.A i,iii). If `max_z nu(z) <= m`, then `Z` and `R` agree up to the factor `m`.
91	
92	- **A high fiber `nu(z)` is a rich linear section.** `nu(z) = #(M_z cap Proj_V Split_L^sf)` is the number of fully-split squarefree degree-`j` locators on one ruling (dim `<= t`) of the reduced moving scroll. A ruling carrying `>> lambda` split locators is precisely a *locator-scroll-section* or a quotient/contained structure — the object of `W-MCA-AA-RES-LOCATOR-SCROLL-SECTION-OR-QUOTIENT`.
93	
94	- **What the hypotheses do.** Balanced minimal indices kill the *geometric* mechanism for a distinguished rich ruling (all indices in `{0,1}` in reserve scale, so no directrix of degree `>= 2`); no-quotient-action-rank-defect kills the *arithmetic* mechanism (`X^M` / bounded-defect `d_M(E)`). So the hypotheses are aimed exactly at `max_z nu(z)`, i.e. at the gap. **If they delivered `max_z nu(z) = n^{o(1)}`, then `Z` and `R` would be comparable up to `n^{o(1)}` and the two walls would coincide.** But (Cycle 50 Route Cut 1) balanced index alone does not exclude bounded-defect quotient packets, so this fiber bound is *not* delivered by the stated hypotheses — it is itself the open scroll-section inverse theorem.
95	
96	Net answer to Q1/Q2. The honest dichotomy:
97	
98	```text
99	- Tangent/core part:  Z_tan = O(j) is unconditionally easier than its R-part (banked template).
100	- Transverse part:    Z_mov-bound and R_mov-bound are NOT separable with present tools.
101	                      Comparability (Z~R) IS what quotient/balanced separation targets, but
102	                      proving it is the open wall, and even Z~R does not upper-bound either
103	                      without a separate handle on R.
104	```

106	So: value-set is strictly easier *only on the tangent core* (which is where multiplicity collapse genuinely lives and is already handled). On the transverse scroll, quotient/tangent separation makes `Z` and `R` *morally* equivalent without giving an upper bound on either.

108	## 5. EXACT_NEW_WALL — the only route that makes `Z` genuinely easier
109	
110	The escape from §3 route (A) is route (B): count distinct slopes directly. The image is
111	
112	```text
113	Z_mov = #{ z in F : ker(H(u)+zH(v)) contains a squarefree fully-L-split monic deg-j locator }.
114	```
115	
116	This is a *splitting-type* count: the number of `z` for which the moving scroll's fiber over `z` meets the totally-split locus of `Split_L^sf`. 118	```text

120

122	separated Hankel pencil over F. Let delta_split(P) be the F-density of slopes z
123	whose moving fiber M_z carries a fully-L-split squarefree degree-j locator.
124	Prove   Z_mov = Q * delta_split(P) + O(sqrt(Q) * deg)   <=   R_line + O(j),
125	i.e. bound the totally-split-fiber density of the degree-<=t moving scroll

The fixed-`t=2,j=4` `S_4` work gave positive split density (the *failure* direction, Cycle 43 cut its reserve-scaling); the balanced-diagonal density decay is the *safe* direction and is the right reserve-scale target.

131	## 6. Counterpacket target (answers Q4)
132	
133	A valid `COUNTERPACKET` must violate the *image* bound, not the landing bound:
134	
135	```text

137	action-rank defect (check d_M(E) for all proper M, not just syntactic X^M pullback),
138	core quotiented to tangent/common-envelope, all incidences transverse (b(T)!=0),

148	Forbidden cheap versions (explicitly not counterpackets): (a) a tangent/common-envelope core with `R >> Z` but `Z <= j+1` — high landing multiplicity, small image; (b) a quotient/bounded-defect packet `E | m(X^M)` realizing `~binom(n/M-1,k/M)` slopes — these have many distinct slopes but are excluded by hypothesis and must be charged to `U_quotient`. 150	## 7. Relation to the Cycle 44 fixed-anchor anticollision wall (answers Q5)
151	
152	They are **dual, on opposite sides of the entropy boundary, and the L2 estimate serves the other side**:
153	
154	- Cycle 44 wants `N_split` (= image `Z`) *large* (the failure/lower branch, `c < H_2(rho)`). It uses `Z >= #Land^2/M_2` (51.A(ii)) and bounds `M_2` from above (the L2-anticollision `M_2 <= #Land + (1+o(1))#Land^2/q_line`). This is the *correct* direction for a lower bound on the image.

The shared object is `nu`; the asymmetry is that L2 is one-directional (51.A(ii)).

162	## 8. Falsification / verification checker (unrun — for the director)
163	
164	Tests 51.A (the sandwich, especially that L2 never upper-bounds `Z`), the core/transverse split, and searches a balanced quotient-separated family for `Z > R_line + Cj` with low-multiplicity excess (a `COUNTERPACKET` seed). I could not execute it here.

166	```python
167	# CHECKER.py — Cycle 51 value-set vs landing (pure Python, small fields)
168	import itertools, random
169	
170	def run(p=7, k=2, t=2, trials=400, C=3):
171	    n=p; r=n-k; j=r-t
172	    assert t==r-j and 0<j<=r
173	    inv=[0]+[pow(a,p-2,p) for a in range(1,p)]
174	    def hankel(w):                       # t x (j+1)
175	        return [[w[m+l] for l in range(j+1)] for m in range(t)]
176	    def loc(T):                          # monic prod (X-x), low->high, len j+1
177	        c=[1]
178	        for x in T:
179	            c=[(-x*c[0])%p]+[(c[i]-x*c[i+1])%p for i in range(len(c)-1)]+[1]
180	        return c
181	    def mv(H,e): return tuple(sum(H[m][l]*e[l] for l in range(j+1))%p for m in range(t))
182	    def slope(a,b):                      # b!=0 ; return z if a||b else None
183	        nz=[i for i in range(t) if b[i]%p]
184	        if not nz: return None
185	        z=(-a[nz[0]]*inv[b[nz[0]]])%p
186	        return z if all((a[i]+z*b[i])%p==0 for i in range(t)) else None
187	    combs=list(itertools.combinations(range(n),j))
188	    Rline=-(-len(combs)//(p**(t-1)))     # ceil(C(n,j)/Q^{t-1}); toy Q=p
189	    bad=0
190	    for _ in range(trials):
191	        u=[random.randrange(p) for _ in range(r+ j)]  # enough syndrome entries
192	        v=[random.randrange(p) for _ in range(r+ j)]
193	        Hu,Hv=hankel(u),hankel(v)
194	        nu={}
195	        for T in combs:
196	            e=loc(T)
197	            a=mv(Hu,e); b=mv(Hv,e)
198	            z=slope(a,b)
199	            if z is not None: nu[z]=nu.get(z,0)+1
200	        R=sum(nu.values()); Z=len(nu); M2=sum(x*x for x in nu.values())
201	        assert Z<=R, "51.A(i) violated (theorem)"
202	        if R>0: assert Z*M2>=R*R, "51.A(ii) violated (theorem)"
203	        # value-set candidate with LOW-multiplicity excess (genuine Z-counterpacket)
204	        if Z>Rline+C*j and (max(nu.values()) if nu else 0)<=2:
205	            bad+=1; print("Z-COUNTERPACKET SEED: Z",Z,"Rline",Rline,"j",j,
206	                           "maxnu",max(nu.values()))
207	    print(f"p={p} k={k} t={t} j={j} Rline={Rline} trials={trials} seeds={bad}")
208	
209	# Real ledger: replace toy Q=p with F_{p^2} (Q=p^2) and compute the slope via the
210	# Cycle 44 residue rho(T)=-ell*Lambda(T)^{-1}*N(T) so R(u,v)=#Land exactly (Lemma 50.C).
211	# A seed counts ONLY if max-multiplicity stays O(1) (else it is a landing artifact, not Z).
212	if __name__=="__main__": run()
213	```
